title: Fix conformance test fixture path assumption (broken standalone)
description: tests/ConformanceTest.php (as committed on origin/main) loads fixtures via __DIR__ . '/../../../../tests/sdk-conformance/cases.json', a path that only resolves if this repo is checked out as a subdirectory of a pdftract monorepo. In the actual standalone pdftract-php repo that directory does not exist, so setUp() calls $this->fail(...) immediately -- every test in the shipped suite fails before it can run. Fix by vendoring the conformance fixtures into this repo (e.g. tests/fixtures/) or fetching them from the pdftract project's release artifacts, and update the path constants accordingly. See docs/plan/plan.md (Known issues) for context. Verify with: composer install && ./vendor/bin/phpunit

title: Add batch/concurrent extraction API once ADR-1 HTTP client lands
description: Once the ADR-1 HTTP-client migration (docs/plan/plan.md) is in place, add a Client::extractBatch(array $sources, array $options = []): array method that fires concurrent HTTP requests (e.g. via curl_multi) instead of forcing callers to loop and await one extract() at a time. This is the kind of batch PDF-processing workload the CLI-subprocess design could never support well (one OS process per call); it becomes cheap once the transport is HTTP. Depends on the HTTP client migration bead landing first -- do not start until Client has a working HTTP transport.

title: Reconcile abandoned working-tree WIP per ADR-1 (HTTP client migration)
description: The lab checkout has ~7 weeks of uncommitted, half-finished work (src/Client.php, src/Codegen/, src/Models/ at the src/ root, plus modified composer.json/README.md/phpunit.xml/tests/ConformanceTest.php) that starts migrating the SDK from a proc_open CLI-subprocess wrapper to an HTTP client, but only reimplements 2 of 25 model classes and leaves a stub Codegen/Methods.php ('This class will be populated with auto-generated methods'). docs/plan/plan.md ADR-1 (2026-07-20) decides the SDK should move to an HTTP client against 'pdftract --serve'. This bead is to execute that decision: finish the HTTP Client implementation, port the remaining model classes and the full exception hierarchy, delete the superseded src/Pdftract/ tree once nothing references it, and fix the composer.json PSR-4 autoload root consistently (src/ vs src/Pdftract/ -- currently the committed and uncommitted trees disagree). Read docs/plan/plan.md ADR-1 in full before starting.

title: Dedupe six proc_open/pipe-handling blocks in src/Pdftract/Client.php
description: In the committed Client.php (src/Pdftract/Client.php), extractText(), extractMarkdown(), extractStream(), search(), and verifyReceipt() each hand-roll their own ~30-line proc_open/escapeshellcmd/pipe-reading block instead of reusing the private exec() helper that extract(), getMetadata(), hash(), and classify() already share. Refactor all methods to go through one shared private transport method (extend exec() to optionally return raw stdout or a streaming generator instead of only decoded JSON). This is useful even independent of the ADR-1 HTTP migration since it's the code as currently shipped on origin/main. Keep behavior identical -- this is a pure refactor, add/extend phpunit coverage to pin current behavior before refactoring.

title: Add CI workflow for pdftract-php (phpunit + composer validate)
description: pdftract-php has no CI configured anywhere -- no workflow files in the repo, and no entry for it in the fleet's Argo WorkflowTemplate table (jedarden/declarative-config k8s/iad-ci/argo-workflows/). This is why the broken conformance-suite fixture path (see the 'Fix conformance test fixture path assumption' bead) went unnoticed. Add a WorkflowTemplate (e.g. pdftract-php-ci) to jedarden/declarative-config following the pattern of forge-ci/needle-ci, triggered on push to jedarden/pdftract-php (Forgejo, git.ardenone.com), running composer validate, composer install, and ./vendor/bin/phpunit. Add it to the WorkflowTemplate table in this workspace's CLAUDE.md once live.

title: Publish jedarden/pdftract on Packagist or fix install instructions
description: README.md instructs 'composer require jedarden/pdftract' but the package is not listed on Packagist (confirmed via search 2026-07-20), so that command fails for any external user with a stock Composer config. Either submit the package to Packagist (packagist.org, requires a stable tagged release and probably the ADR-1 HTTP-client migration + fixed conformance suite landing first so v1.0.0 is something worth publishing) or, in the interim, update README.md's Installation section to the working VCS-repository incantation (composer.json repositories entry pointing at git.ardenone.com/jedarden/pdftract-php) so the documented install path actually works today.

title: Add timeout to Client subprocess/HTTP calls (currently unbounded)
description: No proc_open() call in src/Pdftract/Client.php sets a timeout. A hung or pathologically slow pdftract invocation (e.g. a malformed/huge PDF) blocks the calling PHP process indefinitely, with no way for a caller to bound it. Add a constructor-configurable timeout (e.g. $timeoutSeconds, default a few seconds for metadata/hash, longer for extract/OCR) enforced via stream_select()/proc_terminate() on the child process. If ADR-1's HTTP migration (see docs/plan/plan.md) lands first, apply the equivalent as a curl CURLOPT_TIMEOUT / connect-timeout instead -- whichever transport is current when this is picked up.
